# =============================================================================
# IMPORTS
# =============================================================================

# Standard libraries
import os
import sys
import random
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

# Data manipulation
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from collections import Counter

# Visualization
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from matplotlib.colors import ListedColormap

# Scikit-learn: preprocessing & model selection
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder, LabelEncoder, StandardScaler
from sklearn import model_selection, metrics, preprocessing
from sklearn.model_selection import (
    train_test_split,
    cross_val_score,
    GridSearchCV,
    RandomizedSearchCV,
    TimeSeriesSplit
)
from sklearn.metrics import (
    mean_absolute_error,
    mean_squared_error,
    mean_absolute_percentage_error,
    r2_score
)
from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
from scipy.stats import (
    randint, uniform, pointbiserialr,
    chi2_contingency, spearmanr, entropy
)

# Statistical analysis
from statsmodels.stats.outliers_influence import variance_inflation_factor
from statsmodels.graphics.gofplots import qqplot

# Tabulate
from tabulate import tabulate

# TensorFlow / Keras
import tensorflow as tf
from tensorflow.keras.models import Sequential
from tensorflow.keras.layers import Conv1D, Flatten, Dense, Dropout
from tensorflow.keras.optimizers import Adam

# SciKeras wrapper (for sklearn-compatible CNN)
from scikeras.wrappers import KerasRegressor

# Optuna
import optuna
optuna.logging.set_verbosity(optuna.logging.WARNING)  # Suppress Optuna trial logs

# Google Drive download
import gdown

# Global random seed
RANDOM_SEED = 42
random.seed(RANDOM_SEED)
np.random.seed(RANDOM_SEED)
tf.random.set_seed(RANDOM_SEED)

# Set visualization style
#sns.set()  # Set Seaborn default style
#plt.style.use('ggplot')  # Set ggplot style for matplotlib

# =============================================================================
# LOADING THE DATA SET
# =============================================================================
# Dataset source:
# https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Z_KsoIumw-fvivVombIoWuRo0LOe2nCb/view?usp=sharing
# https://drive.google.com/file/d/1aD1PXfwEEZ_F2lQgxuPfj-TVbxQ6NajK/view?usp=sharing

file_id      = "1qhCB1Dod6jeEsuKy5sNk4MNHSjtENSrx"
download_url = f"https://drive.google.com/uc?id={file_id}"

# Read the data
df = pd.read_csv(download_url)

# Backup original DataFrame
df_backup = df.copy()

# =============================================================================
# DATA PREPROCESSING
# =============================================================================

# Create datetime directly from differently named columns
df['Date'] = pd.to_datetime({
    'year': df['YEAR'],
    'month': df['MO'],
    'day': df['DY']
})

# Set Date as the index
df = df.set_index('Date')

# Drop unnecessary columns
df = df.drop(columns=["YEAR", "MO", "DY"])

# Display the DataFrame (optional in scripts)
print(df.head())
print("\nColumns:", df.columns.tolist())

# =============================================================================
# TRAIN / TEST SPLIT  (80% train — 20% test, temporal order preserved)
# =============================================================================
## Data Sets

# Define features and target
X = df.drop(columns=['WS10M'])   # Features (lagged variables)
y = df['WS10M']                  # Target variable (wind speed)

numerical_cols = df.columns

# Define the split index — 80% for training, 20% for testing
split_index = int(len(X) * 0.8)

# Training set
X_train = X.iloc[:split_index]
y_train = y.iloc[:split_index]

# Testing set
X_test = X.iloc[split_index:]
y_test = y.iloc[split_index:]

print("X_train:\n", X_train.head())
print("y_train:\n", y_train.head())
print("X_test:\n",  X_test.head())
print("y_test:\n",  y_test.head())

print("\nShapes:")
print("X_train shape:", X_train.shape)
print("y_train shape:", y_train.shape)
print("X_test shape: ", X_test.shape)
print("y_test shape: ", y_test.shape)

# =============================================================================
# SCALING
# FIX: Scaler is fit once on X_train only and reused across ALL sections.
#      Fitting a new scaler in each section is redundant and risks inconsistency.
# =============================================================================

# ===== CRITICAL ADDITION: STANDARD SCALING =====
# Standard Scaling — fit on training data only, never on test data
scaler         = StandardScaler()
X_train_scaled = scaler.fit_transform(X_train)   # Fit and transform train
X_test_scaled  = scaler.transform(X_test)        # Apply same scaling to test data

# =============================================================================
# CNN INPUT RESHAPING
# Reshape for Conv1D: (samples, timesteps=1, features)
# All sections use these shared arrays — no redundant reshaping needed.
# =============================================================================

# Reshape for Conv1D: (samples, timesteps=1, features)
X_train_cnn = np.expand_dims(X_train_scaled, axis=1)   # Shape: [samples, 1, features]
X_test_cnn  = np.expand_dims(X_test_scaled,  axis=1)   # Shape: [samples, 1, features]

# Convert targets to arrays
y_train_cnn = y_train.values
y_test_cnn  = y_test.values

print("X_train_cnn shape:", X_train_cnn.shape)
print("X_test_cnn shape: ", X_test_cnn.shape)


# =============================================================================
# EVALUATION FUNCTION  (defined once, reused across all sections)
# =============================================================================

# Function to calculate evaluation metrics
def evaluate_metrics(y_true, y_pred, label=""):
    mse  = mean_squared_error(y_true, y_pred)
    rmse = np.sqrt(mse)
    mae  = mean_absolute_error(y_true, y_pred)
    # Avoid division by zero in MAPE
    mape = np.mean(np.abs((y_true - y_pred) / np.maximum(y_true, 1e-8))) * 100
    r2   = r2_score(y_true, y_pred)
    print(f"{label} MSE:  {mse:.4f}")
    print(f"{label} RMSE: {rmse:.4f}")
    print(f"{label} MAE:  {mae:.4f}")
    print(f"{label} MAPE: {mape:.2f}%")
    print(f"{label} R²:   {r2:.4f}")
    return mse, rmse, mae, mape, r2
## Section 1 — Original CNN Model (No Hyperparameter Optimisation)

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 1 — ORIGINAL CNN MODEL  (No Hyperparameter Optimisation)
# =============================================================================
## CNN Original

# Assuming you have these from before:
# X_train_cnn, X_test_cnn (scaled & reshaped)
# y_train_cnn, y_test_cnn (target variables as numpy arrays)

# Build CNN model
# Conv1D expects input shape = (timesteps, features)
cnn_orig = Sequential()
cnn_orig.add(Conv1D(filters=64, kernel_size=1, activation='relu',
                    input_shape=(X_train_cnn.shape[1], X_train_cnn.shape[2])))
cnn_orig.add(Flatten())          # Flatten before Dense layers
cnn_orig.add(Dense(50, activation='relu'))
cnn_orig.add(Dense(1))           # Output layer for regression

cnn_orig.compile(optimizer=Adam(learning_rate=0.001), loss='mse')

# Train the model
history_orig = cnn_orig.fit(
    X_train_cnn, y_train_cnn,
    epochs=100,
    batch_size=32,
    validation_split=0.2,
    verbose=1,
    shuffle=False   # Critical for time series: do not shuffle
)

# Predict
y_train_pred_cnn = cnn_orig.predict(X_train_cnn).flatten()
y_test_pred_cnn  = cnn_orig.predict(X_test_cnn).flatten()

# Evaluate CNN — store named metric variables for the final comparison table
print("\n--- CNN Original Model Evaluation ---")
cnn_train_mse,  cnn_train_rmse,  cnn_train_mae,  cnn_train_mape,  cnn_train_r2  = \
    evaluate_metrics(y_train_cnn, y_train_pred_cnn, label="Train (CNN)")
cnn_test_mse,   cnn_test_rmse,   cnn_test_mae,   cnn_test_mape,   cnn_test_r2   = \
    evaluate_metrics(y_test_cnn,  y_test_pred_cnn,  label="Test (CNN)")

# Comparison dataframe
comparison_df = pd.DataFrame({
    'Actual':    y_test_cnn,
    'Predicted': y_test_pred_cnn
})
print(comparison_df.head(20))

# Plot results
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.plot(y_test_cnn,      label='Actual',    marker='o')
plt.plot(y_test_pred_cnn, label='Predicted', marker='x')
plt.title('Original CNN — Actual vs Predicted Values (Test Set)')
plt.xlabel('Sample Index')
plt.ylabel('Target Value')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()
## Section 2 — Grid Search CV Hyperparameter Tuning
TimeSeriesSplit cross-validation applied on training data only.

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 2 — GRID SEARCH CV  Hyperparameter Tuning
# TimeSeriesSplit cross-validation applied on training data only
# =============================================================================
## Grid Search

# Build CNN model builder function for KerasRegressor
def build_cnn_model(filters=64, kernel_size=1, dropout_rate=0.2, learning_rate=0.001):
    model = Sequential()
    model.add(Conv1D(filters=filters, kernel_size=kernel_size, activation='relu',
                     input_shape=(X_train_cnn.shape[1], X_train_cnn.shape[2])))
    model.add(Flatten())
    model.add(Dropout(dropout_rate))
    model.add(Dense(50, activation='relu'))
    model.add(Dense(1))
    optimizer = Adam(learning_rate=learning_rate)
    model.compile(loss='mse', optimizer=optimizer)
    return model

# Wrap Keras model for sklearn compatibility
cnn_regressor_grid = KerasRegressor(model=build_cnn_model, verbose=0,
                                    random_state=RANDOM_SEED)

# Parameter grid with correct scikeras model__ prefixes
param_grid = {
    'model__filters':       [32, 64, 128],
    'model__dropout_rate':  [0.1, 0.2, 0.3],
    'model__learning_rate': [1e-2, 1e-3, 1e-4],
    'batch_size':           [16, 32],
    'epochs':               [50, 100]
}

# TimeSeriesSplit ensures training always precedes validation in time
tscv = TimeSeriesSplit(n_splits=5)

# Setup GridSearchCV — fitted on X_train_cnn only (no test set exposure)
grid_search = GridSearchCV(
    estimator=cnn_regressor_grid,
    param_grid=param_grid,
    scoring='neg_mean_squared_error',
    cv=tscv,
    verbose=2,
    n_jobs=1,     # Keep n_jobs=1 for TensorFlow thread safety
    refit=True    # Refit best model on full X_train_cnn automatically
)

# Fit grid search on training data only
grid_search.fit(X_train_cnn, y_train_cnn)

print("\nBest Parameters found (Grid Search):")
print(grid_search.best_params_)

# Best model from grid search
best_cnn_grid = grid_search.best_estimator_

# --- Optimal performance: predict on full X_train_cnn and X_test_cnn ---
y_train_pred_cnn_grid = best_cnn_grid.predict(X_train_cnn).flatten()
y_test_pred_cnn_grid  = best_cnn_grid.predict(X_test_cnn).flatten()

# Evaluate
print("\n--- Optimized CNN Evaluation (Grid Search) ---")
grid_train_mse,  grid_train_rmse,  grid_train_mae,  grid_train_mape,  grid_train_r2  = \
    evaluate_metrics(y_train_cnn, y_train_pred_cnn_grid, label="Train (CNN Grid Search)")
grid_test_mse,   grid_test_rmse,   grid_test_mae,   grid_test_mape,   grid_test_r2   = \
    evaluate_metrics(y_test_cnn,  y_test_pred_cnn_grid,  label="Test (CNN Grid Search)")

# Plot results
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.plot(y_test_cnn,              label='Actual',                     color='black', linewidth=2)
plt.plot(y_test_pred_cnn_grid,    label='Predicted (CNN Grid Search)', color='blue',  linestyle='--', linewidth=2)
plt.title('Grid Search CNN — Actual vs Predicted Values (Test Set)')
plt.xlabel('Observation Index')
plt.ylabel('Target Value')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, linestyle='--', alpha=0.6)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

## Section 3 — Randomized Search CV Hyperparameter Tuning
TimeSeriesSplit cross-validation applied on training data only.  
**Fix 1**: Original used `cv=3` (standard KFold) — data leakage for time series. Replaced with `TimeSeriesSplit(n_splits=5)`.  
**Fix 2**: Original `param_dist` used bare keys (`'filters'`, `'dropout_rate'`, `'learning_rate'`) instead of scikeras-required `model__` prefixes. The tuned values were silently ignored and the model ran with defaults only. Corrected to `'model__filters'`, `'model__dropout_rate'`, `'model__learning_rate'`.

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 3 — RANDOMIZED SEARCH CV  Hyperparameter Tuning
# FIX 1: Original used cv=3 (standard KFold). For a time series problem, standard
#         KFold randomly mixes future observations into training folds — data leakage.
#         Replaced with TimeSeriesSplit(n_splits=5).
# FIX 2: param_dist used bare keys ('filters', 'dropout_rate', 'learning_rate').
#         Scikeras requires model__ prefix for model constructor arguments.
#         Without this, GridSearchCV/RandomizedSearchCV silently ignores the
#         sampled values and uses the defaults — hyperparameter tuning does nothing.
#         Corrected to 'model__filters', 'model__dropout_rate', 'model__learning_rate'.
# TimeSeriesSplit cross-validation applied on training data only
# =============================================================================
## Random Search

# Define CNN model builder function for KerasRegressor
def create_cnn_model(filters=64, dropout_rate=0.2, learning_rate=0.001):
    model = Sequential()
    model.add(Conv1D(filters=filters, kernel_size=1, activation='relu',
                     input_shape=(X_train_cnn.shape[1], X_train_cnn.shape[2])))
    model.add(Flatten())
    model.add(Dropout(dropout_rate))
    model.add(Dense(50, activation='relu'))
    model.add(Dense(1))   # Regression output
    optimizer = Adam(learning_rate=learning_rate)
    model.compile(loss='mse', optimizer=optimizer)
    return model

# Wrap model for sklearn compatibility
cnn_regressor_random = KerasRegressor(
    model=create_cnn_model,
    verbose=0,
    random_state=RANDOM_SEED
)

# Define hyperparameter distribution for RandomizedSearchCV
# FIX: bare keys replaced with model__ prefixes so scikeras correctly passes
#      the sampled values into the model builder function
param_dist = {
    'model__filters':       randint(20, 151),
    'model__dropout_rate':  uniform(0.1, 0.3),
    'model__learning_rate': uniform(1e-4, 9e-3),
    'batch_size':           randint(16, 65),
    'epochs':               randint(50, 151)
}

# TimeSeriesSplit — applied on training data only
# FIX: replaced cv=3 (KFold) with TimeSeriesSplit to prevent data leakage
tscv = TimeSeriesSplit(n_splits=5)

# Setup RandomizedSearchCV — fitted on X_train_cnn only (no test set exposure)
random_search = RandomizedSearchCV(
    estimator=cnn_regressor_random,
    param_distributions=param_dist,
    n_iter=10,
    cv=tscv,
    random_state=RANDOM_SEED,
    verbose=2,
    n_jobs=1,     # Keep n_jobs=1 for TensorFlow thread safety
    refit=True    # Refit best model on full X_train_cnn automatically
)

# Fit RandomizedSearchCV on training data only
random_search.fit(X_train_cnn, y_train_cnn)

# Best parameters
print("Best Hyperparameters (Randomized Search):", random_search.best_params_)

# --- Optimal performance: predict on full X_train_cnn and X_test_cnn ---
y_train_pred_cnn_random = random_search.predict(X_train_cnn).flatten()
y_test_pred_cnn_random  = random_search.predict(X_test_cnn).flatten()

# Evaluation
print("\n--- CNN Evaluation (Randomized Search) ---")
random_train_mse,  random_train_rmse,  random_train_mae,  random_train_mape,  random_train_r2  = \
    evaluate_metrics(y_train_cnn, y_train_pred_cnn_random, label="Train (CNN Randomized Search)")
random_test_mse,   random_test_rmse,   random_test_mae,   random_test_mape,   random_test_r2   = \
    evaluate_metrics(y_test_cnn,  y_test_pred_cnn_random,  label="Test (CNN Randomized Search)")

# Create a DataFrame to compare actual and predicted values on test set
comparison_df = pd.DataFrame({
    'Actual':    y_test_cnn,
    'Predicted': y_test_pred_cnn_random
})
print(comparison_df.head(20))

# Plot actual vs predicted for test set
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.plot(y_test_cnn,                label='Actual',                         color='black', linewidth=2)
plt.plot(y_test_pred_cnn_random,    label='Predicted (CNN Randomized Search)', color='blue', linestyle='--', linewidth=2)
plt.title('Randomized Search CNN — Actual vs Predicted (Test Set)')
plt.xlabel('Sample Index')
plt.ylabel('Target Value')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, linestyle='--', alpha=0.6)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

## Section 4 — Optuna Hyperparameter Tuning
TimeSeriesSplit cross-validation applied on training data only.  
**Fix**: `suggest_loguniform` deprecated — replaced with `suggest_float(..., log=True)`.

# =============================================================================
# SECTION 4 — OPTUNA  Hyperparameter Tuning
# FIX: suggest_loguniform is deprecated — replaced with suggest_float(..., log=True)
# TimeSeriesSplit cross-validation applied on training data only
# =============================================================================
## Optuna

# Build CNN model function for Optuna
def create_cnn_model_optuna(trial):
    filters      = trial.suggest_int('filters', 20, 150)
    dropout_rate = trial.suggest_float('dropout_rate', 0.1, 0.5)
    # FIX: suggest_loguniform is deprecated — replaced with suggest_float(..., log=True)
    learning_rate = trial.suggest_float('learning_rate', 1e-4, 1e-2, log=True)

    model = Sequential()
    model.add(Conv1D(filters=filters, kernel_size=1, activation='relu',
                     input_shape=(X_train_cnn.shape[1], X_train_cnn.shape[2])))
    model.add(Flatten())
    model.add(Dropout(dropout_rate))
    model.add(Dense(50, activation='relu'))
    model.add(Dense(1))   # Regression output
    optimizer = Adam(learning_rate=learning_rate)
    model.compile(loss='mse', optimizer=optimizer)
    return model

# Optuna objective for CNN
def cnn_objective(trial):
    batch_size = trial.suggest_categorical('batch_size', [16, 32, 64])
    epochs     = trial.suggest_int('epochs', 50, 150)

    model = create_cnn_model_optuna(trial)

    # Use TimeSeriesSplit CV on X_train_cnn only — avoids any exposure to held-out X_test
    tscv_optuna = TimeSeriesSplit(n_splits=5)
    mse_scores  = []

    for fold_train_idx, fold_val_idx in tscv_optuna.split(X_train_cnn):
        X_fold_tr  = X_train_cnn[fold_train_idx]
        X_fold_val = X_train_cnn[fold_val_idx]
        y_fold_tr  = y_train_cnn[fold_train_idx]
        y_fold_val = y_train_cnn[fold_val_idx]

        model.fit(
            X_fold_tr, y_fold_tr,
            validation_data=(X_fold_val, y_fold_val),
            epochs=epochs,
            batch_size=batch_size,
            verbose=0,
            shuffle=False   # Critical for time series: do not shuffle
        )
        preds = model.predict(X_fold_val).flatten()
        # Return mean CV MSE across all folds — no test set exposure
        mse_scores.append(mean_squared_error(y_fold_val, preds))

    return np.mean(mse_scores)

# Create and run Optuna study
study = optuna.create_study(
    direction='minimize',
    sampler=optuna.samplers.TPESampler(seed=RANDOM_SEED)
)
study.optimize(cnn_objective, n_trials=30, timeout=600)

print("\nBest Trial:")
print(study.best_trial)
print("\nBest Parameters:")
print(study.best_params)

# --- Optimal performance: refit final model on full X_train_cnn ---
best_params = study.best_params

final_cnn_optuna = Sequential()
final_cnn_optuna.add(Conv1D(filters=best_params['filters'], kernel_size=1,
                            activation='relu',
                            input_shape=(X_train_cnn.shape[1], X_train_cnn.shape[2])))
final_cnn_optuna.add(Flatten())
final_cnn_optuna.add(Dropout(best_params['dropout_rate']))
final_cnn_optuna.add(Dense(50, activation='relu'))
final_cnn_optuna.add(Dense(1))
optimizer = Adam(learning_rate=best_params['learning_rate'])
final_cnn_optuna.compile(loss='mse', optimizer=optimizer)

final_cnn_optuna.fit(
    X_train_cnn, y_train_cnn,
    epochs=best_params['epochs'],
    batch_size=best_params['batch_size'],
    verbose=1,
    shuffle=False   # Critical for time series: do not shuffle
)

# Predict
y_train_pred_cnn_optuna = final_cnn_optuna.predict(X_train_cnn).flatten()
y_test_pred_cnn_optuna  = final_cnn_optuna.predict(X_test_cnn).flatten()

# Evaluate
print("\n--- Optimized CNN Evaluation (Optuna) ---")
optuna_train_mse,  optuna_train_rmse,  optuna_train_mae,  optuna_train_mape,  optuna_train_r2  = \
    evaluate_metrics(y_train_cnn, y_train_pred_cnn_optuna, label="Train (CNN Optuna)")
optuna_test_mse,   optuna_test_rmse,   optuna_test_mae,   optuna_test_mape,   optuna_test_r2   = \
    evaluate_metrics(y_test_cnn,  y_test_pred_cnn_optuna,  label="Test (CNN Optuna)")

# Plot results
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.plot(y_test_cnn,               label='Actual',                  color='black', linewidth=2)
plt.plot(y_test_pred_cnn_optuna,   label='Predicted (CNN Optuna)',   color='blue',  linestyle='--', linewidth=2)
plt.title('Optuna CNN — Actual vs Predicted Values (Test Set)')
plt.xlabel('Observation Index')
plt.ylabel('Target Value')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, linestyle='--', alpha=0.6)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
